title: pittosporum brackenridgei
intro: Pittosporum brackenridgei, also known as Hawaiian cherry or Kūa'ā tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pittosporaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pittosporum brackenridgei include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation. Pittosporum brackenridgei can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves, bark, and roots are used in traditional Hawaiian medicine.
